name: ARM7TDMI
notableFeature: Thumb 16-bit ISA halved code size for ROM-constrained devices

name: Intel StrongARM SA-110
notableFeature: First high-performance ARM for handheld PDAs; used in early iPAQs

name: Motorola DragonBall EZ
notableFeature: Powered the Palm Pilot; extreme low power at 16 MHz

name: Texas Instruments OMAP1
notableFeature: First OMAP: paired ARM application core with dedicated DSP for voice/audio

name: Qualcomm MSM3100
notableFeature: Integrated CDMA baseband, setting the template for Qualcomm's Snapdragon lineage

name: Intel XScale PXA250
notableFeature: 400 MHz at ~500 mW; backbone of Windows Mobile PDAs and early smartphones
